title: agrimonia eupatoria subsp. major
intro: Agrimonia eupatoria subsp. major, also known as agrimony, is a perennial plant belonging to the rose family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin problems, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of agrimony include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Agrimony is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used in topical applications such as creams and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
